summary: Implemented issue #252 final standardization status. The new specbridge-final-standardization-status command aggregates readiness, doctor health, repository health, token/context governance, MCP posture, standard boundaries, 95 percent completion, 5 percent remaining work, categorized remaining gaps, blocked boundaries, recommended next governed contracts, validation expectations, and evidence sources. It is read-only by default and writes only .specbridge/standard-readiness/final-standardization.status.json when OutputPath is explicitly provided with Force as needed. Claude Code was invoked for implementation; its first attempt was blocked by the Claude permission layer, the second left the initial command/tests/docs patch before hitting session limits, and Codex completed hardening, evidence, dashboards, and validations.
validations: claude -p issue-252 implementation attempt: first attempt blocked by Claude Write/Edit permission prompt; second attempt left initial implementation before session limit

powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./scripts/specbridge.ps1 -Command specbridge-final-standardization-status: passed with standardization_completion_pct=95 and remaining_standardization_pct=5
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./scripts/specbridge.ps1 -Command specbridge-doctor -FixPlan -OutputFormat json -Offline: healthy, 0 blockers, 0 warnings
GitHub PR #253 checks: passed (claude-review, validate-foundation, generate-pr-review-report, review-gate)
GitHub PR #253 squash merge: passed with merge commit 29f6db43b8aa9232a492c949d9a8957d97d79888 after explicit user/operator authorization
policy_result: Within policy. The command is deterministic and read-only unless OutputPath is explicitly provided. It does not call the network, mutate GitHub, mutate branches, mutate artifacts by default, launch Claude Code, launch Codex, read secrets, change billing, change authentication or authorization, change databases, change CI/CD security, install dependencies, enforce cleanup, enforce retention, deploy, or touch production. Work stayed inside the issue-252 declared scope after adding ledger and dashboard evidence to the contract.
risk_result: Low. The patch is additive: one new CLI library, one CLI dispatch entry, CLI tests, documentation, governed status artifacts, dashboards, ledger evidence, and audit artifacts. No runtime behavior outside the new command is changed.
unresolved_risks: Branch cleanup enforcement, artifact retention enforcement, mutation-capable MCP tools, network MCP transport, and hosted runtime remain blocked by policy and require future dedicated contracts.
The next serious product-build pilot has not yet run; it is recorded as the first recommended next governed contract.
